rules.source_note: Keeper Rulebook percentile skill and characteristic checks; runtime bounds use percentile-check.json.
Keeper Rulebook Idea roll: a percentile check against the investigator's INT characteristic, used to recall a crucial connection or insight.
Keeper Rulebook Know roll: a percentile check against the investigator's EDU characteristic, used to recall a piece of common or specialized knowledge.
Keeper Rulebook bonus and penalty dice for percentile checks cancel one-for-one, then choose the lowest or highest tens digit from extra tens dice.
Keeper Rulebook p.83 opposed rolls: the opponent's skill sets the required success level. Opponent skill <50 -> Regular, 50-89 -> Hard, 90+ -> Extreme.
Keeper Rulebook Chapter 3 age selection applies only the chosen bracket; runtime calculations use age-adjustments.json.
Keeper Rulebook Chapter 3 Characteristics (pp.30-31): STR/CON/DEX/APP/POW roll 3D6, SIZ/INT/EDU roll 2D6+6, Luck rolls 3D6; multiply each roll by 5 for the full value. Luck is independent of POW.
After determining each characteristic or skill percentage, write the full, half, and fifth values on the investigator sheet; runtime calculations use half-fifth-values.json.
Keeper Rulebook Chapter 3 damage bonus and build table uses STR+SIZ total.
Keeper Rulebook Chapter 3 Movement Rate table compares STR and DEX against SIZ, then applies the age MOV penalty.
Keeper Rulebook Chapter 3 derived HP, Magic points, initial SAN, and default Luck values.
Keeper Rulebook Chapter 3 Table II cross-references Credit Rating and period to determine living standard, cash, assets, and spending level.
A pushed roll requires a changed approach and Keeper-stated consequences before rerolling.
Combined checks use one roll compared against more than one relevant target.
Opposed rolls compare success levels before resolving ties or outcomes.
Combat attacks and fighting maneuvers are resolved as percentile combat checks.
Keeper Rulebook p.166: failing a SAN roll always causes the investigator to lose self-control for a moment; the Keeper chooses an involuntary action.
On-foot chase setup compares adjusted MOV values to assign movement actions.
The playtest chase drill treats chase-internal hazard and barrier checks as not pushable.
Keeper Rulebook p132: each participant makes a CON (foot) or Drive Auto (vehicle) roll; Extreme success +1 MOV, failure -1 MOV, success unchanged for the whole chase.
Keeper Rulebook p132: the chase is not played out if the fleeing adjusted MOV is higher than the pursuer's adjusted MOV.
Keeper Rulebook p133: default starting gap is 2 locations; 1 for a tenser chase; not advised beyond 2.
Keeper Rulebook Table V (pp.145-146): common chase vehicles with MOV, Build, armor, and passenger capacity; looked up by name via get_vehicle_stats.
Keeper Rulebook Table VI (p.147): collision severity tiers (minor/moderate/severe/mayhem/roadkill) with Build damage to the vehicle and HP damage to passengers; resolved by vehicle_collision.
Scenario rewards that change SAN should be recorded as reward dice rather than unproven final-state adjustments.
Damage amounts that change HP should be recorded as damage dice rather than unproven state adjustments.

Keeper Rulebook Table XVII (pp.401-405). Each weapon's damage expression excludes DB; the engine appends the attacker's damage bonus (from damage-bonus-build.json) when adds_damage_bonus is true. Melee weapons add DB; firearms do not.

Keeper Rulebook Chapter 4 Skill List (p.56 / PDF idx 55). Each skill maps to its starting base chance percentage; group skills (G) are listed as separate specializations with their own base chance, modern-only skills are flagged, and uncommon skills (T) are marked.

Keeper Rulebook Chapter 4 Skill List (p.56 / PDF idx 55). Group skills (G) are broken into specializations: Fighting (Brawl 25, Axe 15, Sword 20, Spear 20, Flail 10, Garrote 15, Chainsaw 10, Whip 05), Firearms (Handgun 20, Rifle/Shotgun 25, Bow 15, Heavy Weapons 10, Flamethrower 10, Machine Gun 10, Submachine Gun 15), plus Art and Craft, Science, Language, Pilot, Survival.

Keeper Rulebook p.117-119. Disarm is a fighting maneuver; success transfers the target's weapon to the attacker. Build difference grants penalty dice (penalty_die_per_build_difference) or makes the maneuver impossible (build_difference_impossible_at).

Keeper Rulebook p.117-119. Grapple/restrain is a fighting maneuver; success places the target under ongoing restraint (condition: grappled). The restrained character is held until the attacker releases, is incapacitated, or suffers a major wound; the restrained character may use a maneuver of their own to break free.
Corbitt casts Flesh Ward when investigators enter the house.
Corbitt spends Magic points to attack with the floating knife.
Corbitt spends Magic points to animate his body.
The solo Corbitt temporary-insanity playtest uses the summary bout table.
Corbitt is destroyed when investigators successfully use his own dagger against him.
If Corbitt is conquered and destroyed, each participating investigator gains 1D6 Sanity points.
The Bed Attack damage in the playtest is 1D6+2 after a failed Dodge.
The pushed basement search failure applies 1D4+2 HP damage from the possessed dagger or clutter consequence.
Keeper Rulebook Chapter 3 Sample Occupations (pp.40-41). Each occupation lists credit_rating_range, skill_point_formula, occupational_skills, and era tags ([Lovecraftian], [Modern]).
Keeper Rulebook Ch.9 Casting Spells (pp.177-179). First cast Hard POW, pushable, failed push costs x1D6 MP with HP overspill.
Keeper Rulebook Ch.9 Learning a Spell (pp.176-177). Hard INT roll, 2D6 weeks from tome, 1D8 days from person.
Keeper Rulebook Ch.9 Magic Points (p.176). MP=POW/5 floor, regen 1/hr (2/hr POW>100), after zero costs HP.
Keeper Rulebook Ch.12 Grimoire (pp.258-277). Each spell has cost_mp, cost_pow, cost_sanity, casting_time, source_page.
Keeper Rulebook Ch.11 Tomes of Eldritch Lore (pp.226-235). Each tome has language, author, sanity_loss, cthulhu_mythos_gain, study_weeks, spells.
Keeper Rulebook Ch.11 Using Mythos Tomes (pp.225-226). Initial reading costs SAN; full study yields Mythos skill over weeks.
Keeper Rulebook Ch.14 Monsters (pp.268-339). Each monster has STR/CON/SIZ/DEX/INT/POW, HP, armor, SAN loss, attacks.
Keeper Rulebook Table VII Bouts of Madness-Real Time (p.156). 1D10 results for round-by-round bouts.
Keeper Rulebook Table IX Sample Phobias (p.160). Each phobia has a trigger.
Keeper Rulebook Table X Sample Manias (p.161). Each mania has a trigger.
Keeper Rulebook Ch.16 Appendices Equipment (pp.388-393). Prices for 1920s and modern eras.
Keeper Rulebook Ch.6 Sample Poisons (pp.129-131). Each poison has delivery, damage/effect, onset.
Keeper Rulebook Ch.13 Artifacts and Alien Devices (pp.256-265). Each artifact has description and mechanics.
Keeper Rulebook Chapter 8 Sanity (pp.154-156). Encountering a monster costs SAN: success/failure notation e.g. 1/1D10.
Keeper Rulebook p.167 (Sanity checklist F9): maximum Sanity = 99 minus current Cthulhu Mythos skill.

Keeper Rulebook p.179 becoming a believer: a first-hand Mythos encounter forces belief and costs SAN equal to the current Cthulhu Mythos skill; reading a tome may let the investigator choose not to believe (still gain CM, lose max SAN, but no SAN points lost).
Keeper Rulebook p.93: spend Luck points to lower a roll toward a target number; one Luck point reduces the rolled total by one.
Keeper Rulebook p.93: a group Luck roll uses the lowest individual Luck roll among participating investigators.
Keeper Rulebook p.95 (optional): during the Investigator Development Phase, roll 1D100 against current Luck; higher roll gains 1D10 Luck (cap 99).
Keeper Rulebook pp.94-95: a skill earns one tick per regular, hard, or extreme success; excluded for bonus-die-only successes, opposed-roll losers, and combined-roll participants.
Keeper Rulebook p.95: during the Investigator Development Phase, for each ticked skill roll 1D100; higher than the current skill increases it by 1D10 (cap 90 for SAN-reward-raised skills).
Keeper Rulebook p.95: when a skill reaches 90% or above as the result of a development improvement roll, the investigator gains 2D6 Sanity points (up to maximum Sanity).
Story Director Scoring Engine Layer 2: per-action multiplier by module structure_type (linear_acts, branching_investigation, etc.). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-05-story-director-design.md.
Keeper Rulebook Table XVII Key (p405-406): special-damage tags (Stun/Burn/+DB) and weapon-table markers ((i) impale, *, 1B/2B barrels, rate, era).
Keeper Rulebook p406: Stun — target may not act for 1D6 rounds.
Keeper Rulebook p406: Burn — Luck roll to avoid ignition; minimum damage next round, doubled each round until extinguished.
Time-cost categories for validating LLM time estimates in DirectorPlan.time_advance.
Keeper Rulebook p.164: sanity treatment and recovery paths for indefinite insanity (weekly Psychoanalysis, asylum confinement, self-help).
